behaviors.description: A cell containing =SUM(A1:A10) evaluates to the correct sum of the values in cells A1 through A10.
behaviors.justification: SUM is a pure arithmetic function operating on in-memory cell values. No I/O, subprocess, or external dependencies are involved. A unit test setting up cells with known values and verifying the arithmetic will catch all real failures in the SUM logic (wrong range, arithmetic bugs, type coercion).
behaviors.test_description: Create in-memory cells A1–A10 with values {1, 2, …, 10}. Evaluate =SUM(A1:A10) and verify result is 55. Test edge cases: empty range, single cell, negative numbers, floating-point values, and type coercion.

behaviors.description: A cell referencing another cell that contains a formula (e.g., =B1+1 where B1 is =A1*2) produces the correct cascaded result.
behaviors.justification: Cascaded evaluation tests the dependency resolution and evaluation order within the formula engine. A unit test that builds an in-memory dependency chain (A1 → B1 → C1) and verifies each is evaluated in correct order will catch bugs in graph traversal or stale value reuse. All operations are in-process, in-memory.
behaviors.test_description: Create in-memory cells: A1=5, B1==A1*2, C1==B1+1. Evaluate C1, verify result is 11. Change A1 to 10, recalculate, verify C1 is 21 (uses fresh B1=20, not cached B1=10). Test with deeper chains and diamond dependencies.

behaviors.description: The engine detects a circular dependency (A1 references B1, B1 references A1) and returns a circular reference error instead of looping.
behaviors.justification: Circular dependency detection is a local graph algorithm (cycle detection). A unit test that sets up circular references and verifies an error is raised (not stack overflow or infinite loop) will catch all cycle-detection bugs. No I/O, subprocess, or external dependencies.
behaviors.test_description: Create in-memory cells A1==B1+1 and B1==A1+1. Attempt to evaluate A1. Verify a 'circular reference' error is raised. Test longer cycles (A1 → B1 → C1 → A1), self-references (A1==A1+1), and cycles through cross-sheet references if supported.

behaviors.description: VLOOKUP with an exact match returns the correct value from the lookup table, and returns an error when the key is not found.
behaviors.justification: VLOOKUP is a table lookup function operating on in-memory lookup tables. A unit test that creates lookup tables and verifies correct matches and error handling will catch VLOOKUP logic bugs (wrong column, type coercion, missing keys). No I/O or external dependencies.
behaviors.test_description: Create an in-memory lookup table (names and values). Call VLOOKUP for a key that exists and verify correct value returned. Call VLOOKUP for a key that does not exist and verify 'value not found' error. Test different data types, table sizes, duplicate keys, case sensitivity, and empty tables.

behaviors.description: The IF function correctly handles nested conditions (e.g., =IF(A1>10, IF(A1>20, "high", "medium"), "low")).
behaviors.justification: Nested IF tests the expression parser and conditional evaluator. A unit test with in-memory cells and various nested IF expressions will catch bugs in condition evaluation, operator precedence, or nesting logic. No I/O or external dependencies.
behaviors.test_description: Create in-memory cell A1=15. Evaluate =IF(A1>10, IF(A1>20, "high", "medium"), "low") and verify result is "medium". Test multiple nesting depths, different operators (>, <, ==, !=), type coercion, null/empty branches, and deeply nested structures.

behaviors.description: CONCATENATE joins cell values with the correct separator and handles empty cells by skipping them rather than inserting blank strings.
behaviors.justification: CONCATENATE is a string operation on in-memory cell values. A unit test that sets up cells with various values (including empty) and verifies the join skips empty cells will catch empty-cell-handling bugs (inserting blanks, type coercion errors). No I/O or external dependencies.
behaviors.test_description: Create in-memory cells A1="Hello", A2="", A3="World". Evaluate =CONCATENATE(A1, A2, A3) and verify result is "HelloWorld" (not "Hello World" or "Hello  World"). Test different separators if supported, all-empty ranges, single-cell ranges, and type conversion (numbers, dates, booleans).

behaviors.description: A formula referencing a cell in a different sheet (e.g., =Sheet2!A1+1) resolves correctly when the referenced sheet exists.
behaviors.justification: Cross-sheet references require the formula parser to handle sheet prefixes and the evaluator to look up sheets and cells. A unit test with in-memory sheets will catch bugs in sheet lookup, reference parsing, or cell resolution. All operations are in-process, in-memory.
behaviors.test_description: Create in-memory sheets: Sheet1 with A1=5, Sheet2 with A1=10. In Sheet1, evaluate =Sheet2!A1+1 and verify result is 11. Test non-existent sheets (expect error), invalid cell references, cyclic cross-sheet references, and sheet names with special characters or spaces.

behaviors.description: When a referenced cell's value changes, all dependent formulas are recalculated in the correct topological order.
behaviors.justification: Topological recalculation tests the dependency graph and invalidation strategy. A unit test that modifies a cell and verifies all dependents are recalculated with fresh values (not stale cached values) will catch bugs in cache invalidation or evaluation order. All operations are in-process, in-memory.
behaviors.test_description: Create in-memory cells: A1=5, B1==A1*2, C1==B1+10, D1==A1+C1. Change A1 to 10 and recalculate. Verify B1=20, C1=30, D1=40 (each using fresh dependency values). Test with more complex graphs, multiple independent branches, and cells with multiple dependencies.

behaviors.description: The undo operation after editing a formula restores both the formula text and all previously computed dependent values.
behaviors.justification: Undo is an in-process state management feature. A unit test that edits a formula, triggers undo, and verifies the formula text and dependent values are restored will catch bugs in state capture, snapshot restoration, or recalculation after restore. No I/O or external dependencies are required for the core test.
behaviors.test_description: Create in-memory cells A1=5, B1==A1*2. Edit B1 to ==A1*3 and verify B1 now evaluates to 15. Undo the edit and verify B1 formula is ==A1*2 and evaluates to 10. Test undo/redo sequences, multiple edits, cross-cell edits, and boundary cases (undo at history start, redo at history end).

behaviors.description: Saving a workbook to disk and loading it back produces identical cell values and formulas — no data loss or precision change in floating-point results.
behaviors.justification: This behavior requires real disk I/O. A pure in-process unit test with mocked file I/O would miss bugs in the binary serialization format, floating-point encoding, endianness, buffer management, and file corruption. An Integration test using actual temporary files will catch these failures. The failure modes (data loss, precision loss, format bugs) are invisible to mocks.
behaviors.test_description: Create an in-memory workbook with diverse cell types: integers, floating-point (0.1+0.2), strings, dates, formulas with cross-sheet refs, empty cells, and error values. Save to a temporary file. Load the file back. Verify all cell values match original (with appropriate floating-point tolerance), formulas are preserved, no cells missing. Test large workbooks, special characters, extreme float values, and concurrent saves.
